# Dashboard PRD Plan: Authoring Surfaces

**Source:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md`
**Generated:** 2026-04-25
**Source hash:** `3d098e004503e71a777dcc88d3c19a3be003c32aa8a5de202b1feb75a598cf04`
**Section tasks:** 128
**Context mode:** full source section embedded in every task; no excerpt truncation.
**Quality threshold:** every task must score at least 9.5/10 before implementation begins.

## Purpose
Turn every dashboard PRD section into explicit backend-support work. Even visual/frontend sections must produce backend projection, telemetry, fixture, schema, or explicit no-backend rationale so frontend implementation is easy and stable.

## Global Implementation Rules
- Extend existing modules before creating new ones; only add new route/service files when no canonical owner exists.
- Implement production wiring, not only structs, mocks, or isolated helpers.
- Preserve every extracted detail unless a parity-ledger row explicitly marks it covered or deferred.
- Add persistence, events, auth/safety, dashboard projections, and docs updates whenever the requirement reaches those surfaces.
- A checked box means code, tests, docs, parity ledger, and strict gates are done for that task.

## Primary Target Areas
- `crates/roko-serve/src/routes/plans.rs`
- `crates/roko-serve/src/plan_types.rs`
- `crates/roko-compose/src/`

````markdown
---
````

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.

### DASH-19-S002 -- Why this document matters

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:7` through `18`
**Heading level:** H2
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`, `config-deployment`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
## Why this document matters

Thesis 5 from `02-theses-and-principles.md` says creating is co-equal with consuming. The current dashboard is heavily observation-biased — users can look at things but mostly cannot make them. This document specifies the authoring surfaces that balance the dashboard: the dedicated UIs through which users create new first-class objects in the system.

Each object type the system supports has both a read surface (specified in Section IV) and a write surface (specified here). The read surfaces live under specific sidebar sections; the write surfaces are reached from those sections via explicit "Create" actions and from the top-level command palette.

This document groups the authoring surfaces by object type. For each, it specifies: what the surface is for, what primitives it composes, what fields it requires, how validation works, what preview and test modes exist, and how the created object gets persisted (locally, on-chain, or both).

Authoring surfaces share many design patterns. The document starts with those shared patterns and then specifies each surface.

---
````

- Thesis 5 from `02-theses-and-principles.md` says creating is co-equal with consuming. The current dashboard is heavily observation-biased — users can look at things but mostly cannot make them. This document specifies the authoring surfaces that balance the dashboard: the dedicated UIs through which users create new first-class objects in the system.
- Each object type the system supports has both a read surface (specified in Section IV) and a write surface (specified here). The read surfaces live under specific sidebar sections; the write surfaces are reached from those sections via explicit "Create" actions and from the top-level command palette.
- This document groups the authoring surfaces by object type. For each, it specifies: what the surface is for, what primitives it composes, what fields it requires, how validation works, what preview and test modes exist, and how the created object gets persisted (locally, on-chain, or both).

### DASH-19-S004 -- The authoring shell

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:23` through `33`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `config-deployment`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### The authoring shell

Every authoring surface has a consistent shell:

- A header with the authoring title ("New Agent," "New Arena," etc.) and a small breadcrumb showing where the user came from.
- A main area with the authoring form, usually organized into sections with visible structure.
- A live preview panel (right side or below, depending on the object type) showing what the object will look like when deployed.
- A footer with actions: Save Draft, Preview, Deploy (or Publish, Register, depending on the object type), Cancel.

The shell is consistent across objects so users who have authored one object can orient quickly in authoring another.
````

**Explicit detail extraction from this section:**

- Section word count: `100`
- Section hash: `3a51350b5f290cd5c76491233bdefafb5fdcdb816af34e57b1045a5871b7d932`

### DASH-19-S005 -- Start from a template, not a blank slate

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:34` through `45`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `config-deployment`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Start from a template, not a blank slate

Every authoring surface offers templates as a starting point. Blank slates are allowed but discouraged — a user clicking "New Agent" without specifying a template lands on a "Pick a starting point" screen with a few relevant templates plus an explicit "Start from blank" option.

Templates come from three sources:

- System-provided templates (curated, always available).
- User-published templates (community contributions).
- The user's own saved templates (their own prior work).

Forking a template preserves lineage — the new object's metadata records the template it was forked from.
````

**Explicit detail extraction from this section:**

- Section word count: `87`
- Section hash: `1c6e65171532bd19c79298f84cabbb3b885de7b900d106da7db8c68a98c7647c`

### DASH-19-S006 -- Live preview

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:46` through `51`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Live preview

Every authoring surface has a live preview. As the user edits fields, the preview updates. Preview shows what the object will look like in the read surface, or what its behavior will be (for agents, a simulation of one tick; for evals, a test application).

Preview is not instant compile — it debounces on typing and updates as fields settle. No preview is ever "missing" — if the configuration is invalid, the preview shows an error state with the specific issue.
````

- Section word count: `79`
- Section hash: `832fca823c99fd85ed6d5927829a0f576769bf525cad45329ff0d42acbe01db4`

- Every authoring surface has a live preview. As the user edits fields, the preview updates. Preview shows what the object will look like in the read surface, or what its behavior will be (for agents, a simulation of one tick; for evals, a test application).
- Preview is not instant compile — it debounces on typing and updates as fields settle. No preview is ever "missing" — if the configuration is invalid, the preview shows an error state with the specific issue.

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.

### DASH-19-S007 -- Validation surfaces

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:52` through `61`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`, `config-deployment`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Validation surfaces

Authoring validates as the user edits. Three validation levels:

- **Errors** (block save/deploy): structural problems, missing required fields, invalid values. Shown inline with clear messages.
- **Warnings** (don't block but flag): suboptimal but valid configurations ("this gate pipeline may take >5 minutes per task"). Shown inline.
- **Suggestions** (advisory): recommendations based on best practices ("consider adding a test gate to this pipeline"). Shown in a sidebar.

Errors get red treatment, warnings amber, suggestions rose. No surprises when the user tries to deploy — they've seen all issues throughout authoring.
````

**Explicit detail extraction from this section:**

- Section word count: `88`
- Section hash: `3ae2bd22a975640b1a94c06667f0497bca3d65ddcf5e0cdc9abcfce15a9ba395`

Shown inline with clear messages. - **Warnings** (don't block but flag): suboptimal but valid configurations ("this gate pipeline may take >5 minutes per task"). Shown inline. - **Suggestions** (advisory): recommendations based on best practices ("consider adding a test gate to this pipeline"). Shown in a sidebar.

### DASH-19-S008 -- Draft persistence

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:62` through `67`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `dashboard-support`, `config-deployment`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Draft persistence

Authoring surfaces save drafts automatically. A user who closes the tab mid-authoring returns to the same state. Drafts are listed in a "My drafts" section reachable from each authoring surface.

Drafts are local until deployment. Deployment (or publishing to chain) is an explicit action that costs gas/daeji if on-chain and is not automatic.
````

### DASH-19-S009 -- Version control concepts

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:68` through `73`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Version control concepts

Objects can be versioned. Once deployed, an authoring session's output becomes version 1. Subsequent edits create version 2. Users can roll back to prior versions.

Versioning is object-type-specific. Agents have simple versions (configurations). Templates, domains, arenas, and evals have full versioning with semantic versions (major, minor, patch) because other users may pin to specific versions.
````

- Objects can be versioned. Once deployed, an authoring session's output becomes version 1. Subsequent edits create version 2. Users can roll back to prior versions.

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.

### DASH-19-S010 -- Deploy, publish, register

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:74` through `83`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Deploy, publish, register

Three distinct terminal actions:

- **Deploy**: make it live for my fleet (agents, templates I use locally).
- **Publish**: make it available to the network (templates, domains, arenas, evals). On-chain registration happens here.
- **Register**: the specific act of obtaining a chain-native identifier (ERC-8004 for agents, chain object registration for public objects). Often bundled with Publish.

Each action has clear cost implications (gas, daeji stake) that are shown before the user confirms.
````

**Explicit detail extraction from this section:**

- Section hash: `dae49f57f25214eb83089c4171b18ff6ac1f46760fa576014f1808ab0346d86d`

- - **Deploy**: make it live for my fleet (agents, templates I use locally). - **Publish**: make it available to the network (templates, domains, arenas, evals). On-chain registration happens here. - **Register**: the specific act of obtaining a chain-native identifier (ERC-8004 for agents, chain object registration for public objects). Often bundled with Publish.

### DASH-19-S011 -- Permissions

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:84` through `87`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `dashboard-support`, `config-deployment`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Permissions

The user authoring an object becomes its owner. Ownership can later be transferred via explicit action. Until transferred, only the owner can modify; others can read (if public) or not (if private).
````

### DASH-19-S012 -- Collaboration

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:88` through `93`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `dashboard-support`, `config-deployment`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Collaboration

Multiple users can co-author an object if the object is configured for collaboration. Collaboration state (who is editing now, who commented, who approved) is visible in the authoring shell. Collaboration is a deferred feature for most surfaces; basic single-author flows are the MVP.

---
````

- Section hash: `b0b0f1c34943e52d47e8674d4001be3f7a0603698b357fb9e1c725a6e73aed05`

- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

**Explicit implementation obligations derived from this section:**
- [ ] Convert every normative sentence and bullet in the section into ledger-backed implementation tasks; if no backend change is required, write an explicit `covered_by` or `deferred` row with rationale.
- [ ] For this dashboard-derived task, prefer backend projection/realtime support over frontend stitching: one stable payload, typed stale/degraded states, and fixtures for local UI development.

**General implementation checklist:**
- [ ] Add one parity-ledger row for this exact source section with task id, source hash, owner, targets, tests, and acceptance gate.
- [ ] Extend existing canonical modules before adding new modules; document any new module choice in the ledger.
- [ ] Implement production-path wiring across runtime/service/storage/API/events, not only structs or mocks.
- [ ] Add dashboard-friendly projection and realtime payloads when any UI, operator, or PRD requirement would otherwise need to stitch state client-side.
- [ ] Persist durable state under existing storage conventions and cover restart/replay/recovery behavior.
- [ ] Add tests for happy path, invalid input, invalid state, unauthorized access, dependency unavailable, persistence/restart, serialization, and degraded frontend state where applicable.
- [ ] Update generated API/CLI/docs references or record an explicit no-public-surface rationale.
- [ ] Run verification commands and attach produced gate artifacts to the ledger row.

```bash
cargo test -p roko-runtime
cargo test -p roko-chain
cd contracts && forge test
cargo test -p roko-cli
./target/debug/roko parity check --strict --area dashboard-prd/19-authoring-surfaces
```

### DASH-19-S017 -- Stages

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:110` through `172`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Stages

The Composer is a multi-stage flow but not a strict wizard — users can jump between stages.

**Stage 1 — Starting point.**

Pick a template or start blank. Templates shown with the same card style as the Templates page. Filter by domain, tier, popularity. A "Start blank" option at the bottom.

**Stage 2 — Identity.**

- Agent name (free text, with suggestions).
- Short description (optional).
- Visual: the generative agent portrait previews as the user types (portrait is deterministic from the agent's eventual HDC fingerprint, which depends on configuration, so the preview updates as the user configures).

**Stage 3 — Domain.**

- Domain selector (coding, blockchain, research, security, or user-created).
- The domain choice pre-fills recommended extensions, gates, and context strategy.
- A "Customize domain" toggle exposes the domain profile fields for fine-tuning without leaving the composer.

**Stage 4 — Capabilities and extensions.**

- A list of available extensions, grouped by layer (Foundation, Perception, Memory, Cognition, Action, Social, Meta, Recovery).
- Checkboxes to include.
- Required extensions from the domain are pre-checked and non-removable (with tooltip explaining why).
- Each extension shows its hook profile (what parts of the lifecycle it affects), cost implication, and recommended parameter defaults.

**Stage 5 — Gates.**

- The gate pipeline editor (matches Gates page functionality).
- Default pipeline from the domain; user can add, remove, reorder.
- For each gate, show cost, latency, and typical pass rate.

**Stage 6 — Model routing.**

- Default model (for T2 cognition).
- Fast tier model (for T1).
- Standard tier model (for general use).
- Cascade router configuration: let it learn (default) or force specific allocations.
- Cost estimates based on projected tick rate.

**Stage 7 — Budget and caveats.**

- Daily spend cap.
- Per-task spend cap.
- Chain operation caveats (allowed tx types, position size limits).
- Tool caveats (which tools the agent may invoke).
- Withdraw restrictions.

**Stage 8 — Heartbeat and lifecycle.**

- Is this a long-running or ephemeral agent?
- For long-running: gamma/theta intervals, dream cycle frequency.
- Auto-restart behavior on failure.
- Opt-in mortality (opt-out by default).

**Stage 9 — Deployment.**

- Deployment target: local, Nunchi Cloud, user-provided infrastructure.
- Registration: register on-chain now (mint ERC-8004 passport) or later.
- Final review card: shows the complete configuration.
- Deploy button with estimated startup cost.
````

- Section hash: `a23f9c71fd5375349fe90fb464f5b6ca77fa5c3bf191ad482232222259206f72`

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

**Explicit implementation obligations derived from this section:**
- [ ] Convert every normative sentence and bullet in the section into ledger-backed implementation tasks; if no backend change is required, write an explicit `covered_by` or `deferred` row with rationale.
- [ ] For this dashboard-derived task, prefer backend projection/realtime support over frontend stitching: one stable payload, typed stale/degraded states, and fixtures for local UI development.

### DASH-19-S023 -- Purpose

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:201` through `204`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`, `config-deployment`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Purpose

Where users author new extensions. An extension is a Rust crate that implements a subset of the 22 lifecycle hooks. The Workshop wraps the development flow: pick hooks, scaffold the crate, edit the code, test against the hook lifecycle, publish.
````

### DASH-19-S026 -- Stages

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:214` through `252`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Stages

**Stage 1 — Starting point.**

Pick a template extension (examples: a simple gate extension, a perception extension, a cognition extension with affect reading) or start from a minimal skeleton.

**Stage 2 — Identity and layer.**

- Extension name.
- Description.
- Layer (L0-L7).
- Dependencies on other extensions.

**Stage 3 — Hook selection.**

- Choose which of the 22 hooks this extension implements.
- For each hook, a template stub is generated.
- Hook descriptions explain what each is for.

**Stage 4 — Code editing.**

A code editor (inline Monaco or similar) where users write the Rust extension code. The Workshop can be a thin frontend to a proper IDE — clicking "Open in VSCode" launches the code in the user's preferred environment with a server-side project scaffold.

For users who want to stay in the browser, basic editing is supported.

**Stage 5 — Test against fixture.**

A test harness that runs the extension against a fixture agent. User sees:

- Hook firing events.
- State changes the extension produced.
- Any errors or panics.

**Stage 6 — Publish.**

- Compile the extension.
- Publish to user's extension registry (local) or to the network (public).
- Register on-chain if public (for discoverability).
````

**Explicit detail extraction from this section:**

- Section word count: `187`
- Section hash: `5736eadb22b6cfd9d2a093a7935d9354f49b9ddb7984a4f2bd99479598e038c4`

- **Stage 4 — Code editing.**
- A code editor (inline Monaco or similar) where users write the Rust extension code. The Workshop can be a thin frontend to a proper IDE — clicking "Open in VSCode" launches the code in the user's preferred environment with a server-side project scaffold.
- **Stage 5 — Test against fixture.**
- A test harness that runs the extension against a fixture agent. User sees:
- - Hook firing events. - State changes the extension produced. - Any errors or panics.
- **Stage 6 — Publish.**
- - Compile the extension. - Publish to user's extension registry (local) or to the network (public). - Register on-chain if public (for discoverability).

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

**Explicit implementation obligations derived from this section:**
- [ ] Convert every normative sentence and bullet in the section into ledger-backed implementation tasks; if no backend change is required, write an explicit `covered_by` or `deferred` row with rationale.
- [ ] For this dashboard-derived task, prefer backend projection/realtime support over frontend stitching: one stable payload, typed stale/degraded states, and fixtures for local UI development.

**General implementation checklist:**
- [ ] Add one parity-ledger row for this exact source section with task id, source hash, owner, targets, tests, and acceptance gate.
- [ ] Extend existing canonical modules before adding new modules; document any new module choice in the ledger.
- [ ] Implement production-path wiring across runtime/service/storage/API/events, not only structs or mocks.
- [ ] Add dashboard-friendly projection and realtime payloads when any UI, operator, or PRD requirement would otherwise need to stitch state client-side.
- [ ] Persist durable state under existing storage conventions and cover restart/replay/recovery behavior.
- [ ] Add tests for happy path, invalid input, invalid state, unauthorized access, dependency unavailable, persistence/restart, serialization, and degraded frontend state where applicable.
- [ ] Update generated API/CLI/docs references or record an explicit no-public-surface rationale.
- [ ] Run verification commands and attach produced gate artifacts to the ledger row.

**Verification commands:**

```bash
cargo test -p roko-serve
cargo test -p roko-runtime
```
```bash
cargo test -p roko-gate
cargo test -p roko-chain
cd contracts && forge test
cargo test -p roko-cli
./target/debug/roko parity check --strict --area dashboard-prd/19-authoring-surfaces
```

````markdown
### Purpose

Author new gates. A gate is a verification step that checks an agent's output against external truth. Examples: a custom test runner, a specific lint rule, a chain state check, a formal verification pass.
````

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

**Explicit implementation obligations derived from this section:**
- [ ] Enforce state transition `System -> Gates` with invalid-transition rejection tests and persistence/restart behavior.
- [ ] For this dashboard-derived task, prefer backend projection/realtime support over frontend stitching: one stable payload, typed stale/degraded states, and fixtures for local UI development.

**General implementation checklist:**
- [ ] Add one parity-ledger row for this exact source section with task id, source hash, owner, targets, tests, and acceptance gate.
- [ ] Extend existing canonical modules before adding new modules; document any new module choice in the ledger.
- [ ] Implement production-path wiring across runtime/service/storage/API/events, not only structs or mocks.
- [ ] Add dashboard-friendly projection and realtime payloads when any UI, operator, or PRD requirement would otherwise need to stitch state client-side.
- [ ] Persist durable state under existing storage conventions and cover restart/replay/recovery behavior.
- [ ] Add tests for happy path, invalid input, invalid state, unauthorized access, dependency unavailable, persistence/restart, serialization, and degraded frontend state where applicable.
- [ ] Update generated API/CLI/docs references or record an explicit no-public-surface rationale.
- [ ] Run verification commands and attach produced gate artifacts to the ledger row.

**Verification commands:**

```bash

cargo test -p roko-runtime
cargo test -p roko-gate
cargo test -p roko-chain
cd contracts && forge test
cargo test -p roko-cli
./target/debug/roko parity check --strict --area dashboard-prd/19-authoring-surfaces
```

### DASH-19-S035 -- Stages

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:292` through `335`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`, `config-deployment`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Stages

**Stage 1 — Starting point.**

Template gates: compile, test, clippy-warning, chain-sim, custom shell command, custom Rust function.

**Stage 2 — Identity and category.**

- Gate name.
- Category (Build, Test, Lint, Chain, Security, Custom).
- Description.

**Stage 3 — Execution definition.**

How the gate runs:

- For shell gates: the command to run.
- For Rust function gates: the crate path and function signature.
- For chain gates: the chain query or simulation to execute.
- For custom: arbitrary executor configuration.

Input schema: what does the gate receive?

Output schema: pass/fail plus optional detail.

**Stage 4 — Cost and latency profile.**

- Estimated cost per invocation.
- Typical latency.
- These help users understand when to put this gate in their pipelines.

**Stage 5 — Test against fixtures.**

A harness that runs the gate against sample inputs. User sees:

- Pass/fail verdicts.
- Latency.
- Any errors.

**Stage 6 — Publish.**

- Publish to user's gate registry.
- Publish to network for discoverability.
````

- Section hash: `825e0b6d88b65d0b9720aa3e08393660ec5ae70077d00babba65605ebe6b4e0d`

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

**Explicit implementation obligations derived from this section:**
- [ ] Convert every normative sentence and bullet in the section into ledger-backed implementation tasks; if no backend change is required, write an explicit `covered_by` or `deferred` row with rationale.
- [ ] For this dashboard-derived task, prefer backend projection/realtime support over frontend stitching: one stable payload, typed stale/degraded states, and fixtures for local UI development.

- [ ] Add one parity-ledger row for this exact source section with task id, source hash, owner, targets, tests, and acceptance gate.
- [ ] Extend existing canonical modules before adding new modules; document any new module choice in the ledger.

- [ ] Add tests for happy path, invalid input, invalid state, unauthorized access, dependency unavailable, persistence/restart, serialization, and degraded frontend state where applicable.

### DASH-19-S041 -- Purpose

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:360` through `363`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Purpose

Define a new domain. A domain is a declarative specification of an agent type: which extensions, which gates, which context strategy, which model preferences. Published domains become templates other users can adopt.
````

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.

### DASH-19-S044 -- Stages

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:374` through `426`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Stages

**Stage 1 — Starting point.**

Fork an existing domain or start blank. Existing domains visible from Templates and Fleet.

**Stage 2 — Identity.**

- Domain name.
- Category.
- Description (long form, serif italic).
- Intended use cases.

**Stage 3 — Extensions.**

- Required extensions (always loaded by agents in this domain).
- Recommended extensions (loaded by default, user can remove).
- Optional extensions (shown in the picker when agents configure).

**Stage 4 — Gates.**

- Default gate pipeline.
- Per-task gate selection logic (if different tasks in the domain need different gates).

**Stage 5 — Context strategy.**

- Default context budget (token cap per tick).
- Section allocations (role, workspace, plan, knowledge, volatile).
- Retrieval strategy for the knowledge section.
- Decay policy.

**Stage 6 — Model preferences.**

- Default tier models (T0, T1, T2).
- Cost sensitivity.
- Latency tolerance.

**Stage 7 — Heartbeat schedule.**

- Default gamma, theta, delta intervals.
- Dream cycle frequency.

**Stage 8 — Metadata.**

- Tags.
- Version number.
- License (if applicable).

**Stage 9 — Publish.**

- Publish to network (on-chain registration).
- Set versioning policy.
````

**Explicit detail extraction from this section:**

- Section word count: `154`
- Section hash: `ed8c331fafd5cd6f797cd12d059ca934f338aa9f57f89cb2f6aba1a378cfac35`

**Normative requirements and implementation claims:**
- **Stage 1 — Starting point.**
- **Stage 2 — Identity.**
- - Domain name. - Category. - Description (long form, serif italic). - Intended use cases.
- **Stage 3 — Extensions.**
- - Required extensions (always loaded by agents in this domain). - Recommended extensions (loaded by default, user can remove). - Optional extensions (shown in the picker when agents configure).
- **Stage 4 — Gates.**
- - Default gate pipeline. - Per-task gate selection logic (if different tasks in the domain need different gates).
- **Stage 5 — Context strategy.**
- - Default context budget (token cap per tick). - Section allocations (role, workspace, plan, knowledge, volatile). - Retrieval strategy for the knowledge section. - Decay policy.
- **Stage 6 — Model preferences.**
- - Default tier models (T0, T1, T2). - Cost sensitivity. - Latency tolerance.
- **Stage 7 — Heartbeat schedule.**
- - Default gamma, theta, delta intervals. - Dream cycle frequency.
- **Stage 8 — Metadata.**
- - Tags. - Version number. - License (if applicable).
- **Stage 9 — Publish.**
- - Publish to network (on-chain registration). - Set versioning policy.

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.

**Explicit implementation obligations derived from this section:**
- [ ] Convert every normative sentence and bullet in the section into ledger-backed implementation tasks; if no backend change is required, write an explicit `covered_by` or `deferred` row with rationale.
- [ ] For this dashboard-derived task, prefer backend projection/realtime support over frontend stitching: one stable payload, typed stale/degraded states, and fixtures for local UI development.

**General implementation checklist:**
- [ ] Add one parity-ledger row for this exact source section with task id, source hash, owner, targets, tests, and acceptance gate.
- [ ] Extend existing canonical modules before adding new modules; document any new module choice in the ledger.
- [ ] Implement production-path wiring across runtime/service/storage/API/events, not only structs or mocks.
- [ ] Add dashboard-friendly projection and realtime payloads when any UI, operator, or PRD requirement would otherwise need to stitch state client-side.
- [ ] Persist durable state under existing storage conventions and cover restart/replay/recovery behavior.
- [ ] Add tests for happy path, invalid input, invalid state, unauthorized access, dependency unavailable, persistence/restart, serialization, and degraded frontend state where applicable.
- [ ] Update generated API/CLI/docs references or record an explicit no-public-surface rationale.
- [ ] Run verification commands and attach produced gate artifacts to the ledger row.

**Verification commands:**

```bash
cargo test -p roko-serve
cargo test -p roko-runtime
cargo test -p roko-gate
cargo test -p roko-chain
cd contracts && forge test
./target/debug/roko parity check --strict --area dashboard-prd/19-authoring-surfaces
```

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

### DASH-19-S050 -- Purpose

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:452` through `455`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Purpose

Publish a specific agent configuration as a reusable template. Distinct from Domain Builder (which defines a domain as a class). A template is a specific instantiation — a specific agent configuration, with specific model choices, specific extensions, specific gates, ready to fork.
````

- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.

**Explicit implementation obligations derived from this section:**
- [ ] Convert every normative sentence and bullet in the section into ledger-backed implementation tasks; if no backend change is required, write an explicit `covered_by` or `deferred` row with rationale.
- [ ] For this dashboard-derived task, prefer backend projection/realtime support over frontend stitching: one stable payload, typed stale/degraded states, and fixtures for local UI development.

- [ ] Extend existing canonical modules before adding new modules; document any new module choice in the ledger.

### DASH-19-S053 -- Stages

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:465` through `493`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `config-deployment`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Stages

**Stage 1 — Select source.**

Pick an existing agent (of yours) to publish as a template, or start from scratch.

**Stage 2 — Review configuration.**

The full configuration of the source agent is shown. User can redact or modify fields before publishing.

**Stage 3 — Metadata.**

- Template name.
- Description.
- Tags.
- Recommended use cases.
- Credit / attribution (if forked).

**Stage 4 — Visibility.**

- Private (only you).
- Public unlisted (accessible by link).
- Public listed (in the template catalog).

**Stage 5 — Publish.**

- Publish to template registry.
- If public, register on-chain for discoverability.
````

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

- [ ] Convert every normative sentence and bullet in the section into ledger-backed implementation tasks; if no backend change is required, write an explicit `covered_by` or `deferred` row with rationale.

### DASH-19-S062 -- Stages

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:529` through `591`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Stages

**Stage 1 — Starting point.**

Pick an arena template (SWE-bench-style, AMM-style, prediction-market-style, chess, persuasion, custom). Each template pre-fills task source and scoring.

**Stage 2 — Identity.**

- Arena name.
- Category.
- Description (long form).
- Intended participant types.

**Stage 3 — Task source.**

Where do tasks come from?

- Static dataset: upload or reference a dataset.
- Procedural generator: configure a task-generation agent or script.
- User-contributed stream: accept task submissions from users.
- Adversarial: another agent generates tasks.

Each task source has its own configuration.

**Stage 4 — Gate configuration.**

What gates must attempts pass?

- Correctness gates (automated verification).
- Quality gates (scoring).
- Safety gates (disqualification rules).

**Stage 5 — Scoring.**

- Scoring function: pass/fail, continuous, probabilistic (CRPS), custom.
- Ground truth source (MUST be declared per thesis 4).
- Aggregation rule: best of N, mean, median.
- Score bounds.

**Stage 6 — Leaderboard.**

- Global or scoped leaderboard.
- Tie-breaking rules.
- Update frequency.

**Stage 7 — Economics.**

- Prize pool (if any).
- Daeji stake requirements for participants.
- Bounty integration.

**Stage 8 — Metadata.**

- Tags.
- Start date and end date (if time-limited).
- Eligibility rules.

**Stage 9 — Publish.**

- Publish to network.
- Register on-chain.
- Prize pool escrow if applicable.
````

**Explicit detail extraction from this section:**

- Section hash: `b9106ee49ae457cc7f5128e73a1ea5db0eee906206afebcbb819d8658f2f92bf`

- `crates/roko-serve/src/state.rs`

- `apps/mirage-rs/src/`
- `crates/roko-serve/src/routes/projections.rs`

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

**Explicit implementation obligations derived from this section:**
- [ ] Convert every normative sentence and bullet in the section into ledger-backed implementation tasks; if no backend change is required, write an explicit `covered_by` or `deferred` row with rationale.
- [ ] For this dashboard-derived task, prefer backend projection/realtime support over frontend stitching: one stable payload, typed stale/degraded states, and fixtures for local UI development.

**General implementation checklist:**
- [ ] Add one parity-ledger row for this exact source section with task id, source hash, owner, targets, tests, and acceptance gate.
- [ ] Extend existing canonical modules before adding new modules; document any new module choice in the ledger.
- [ ] Implement production-path wiring across runtime/service/storage/API/events, not only structs or mocks.
- [ ] Add dashboard-friendly projection and realtime payloads when any UI, operator, or PRD requirement would otherwise need to stitch state client-side.
- [ ] Persist durable state under existing storage conventions and cover restart/replay/recovery behavior.
- [ ] Add tests for happy path, invalid input, invalid state, unauthorized access, dependency unavailable, persistence/restart, serialization, and degraded frontend state where applicable.
- [ ] Update generated API/CLI/docs references or record an explicit no-public-surface rationale.
- [ ] Run verification commands and attach produced gate artifacts to the ledger row.

```bash

cargo test -p roko-runtime
cargo test -p roko-chain
cd contracts && forge test
cargo test -p roko-cli
./target/debug/roko parity check --strict --area dashboard-prd/19-authoring-surfaces
```

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**

- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.

- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

````markdown
### Purpose

Create a new eval. An eval measures agent behavior against ground truth. Per thesis 4, every eval must declare its ground truth source and must not use the LLM as its primary grader.
````

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

### DASH-19-S071 -- Stages

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:632` through `696`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Stages

**Stage 1 — Starting point.**

Pick an eval template or start blank. Templates organized by ground truth source.

**Stage 2 — Identity.**

- Eval name.
- Description.
- Domain.
- Tags.

**Stage 3 — What is measured.**

- What quantity does this eval produce?
- Units (if applicable).
- Interpretation guidance.

**Stage 4 — Ground truth source.**

This is the critical, blocking stage. User must select from:

- **Gate outcome** (pass/fail of a gate pipeline).
- **Arena result** (score in a specific arena).
- **Chain state** (a specific on-chain query).
- **Oracle value** (an oracle-published value).
- **User judgment** (a human reviewer's verdict).
- **Composed** (a combination of above).

For each, additional configuration: which gate, which arena, which oracle, etc.

If user tries to skip or specify "LLM grader," the UI blocks with an explanation of why this is not allowed.

**Stage 5 — Scoring function.**

How does the ground truth become a score?

- For pass/fail truth: boolean score or weighted boolean.
- For continuous truth: linear, thresholded, exponential, custom.
- For probabilistic: CRPS or Brier score.
- Custom functions: user provides a function (inline or linked).

**Stage 6 — Input spec.**

What does the eval take as input?

- Target type (agent, attempt, configuration, knowledge entry, etc.).
- Required fields.
- Optional fields.

**Stage 7 — Calibration plan.**

How will the eval be validated?

- Initial calibration fixtures.
- Expected calibration quality.
- How frequently to recalibrate.

**Stage 8 — Publish.**

- Publish to Evals Library.
- Register on-chain for discoverability.
- Optionally, open to meta-eval evaluation.
````

- **Stage 5 — Scoring function.**
- - For pass/fail truth: boolean score or weighted boolean. - For continuous truth: linear, thresholded, exponential, custom. - For probabilistic: CRPS or Brier score. - Custom functions: user provides a function (inline or linked).
- **Stage 6 — Input spec.**

- **Stage 8 — Publish.**
- - Publish to Evals Library. - Register on-chain for discoverability. - Optionally, open to meta-eval evaluation.

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

- [ ] For this dashboard-derived task, prefer backend projection/realtime support over frontend stitching: one stable payload, typed stale/degraded states, and fixtures for local UI development.

- [ ] Add one parity-ledger row for this exact source section with task id, source hash, owner, targets, tests, and acceptance gate.

```bash
cd contracts && forge test
cargo test -p roko-cli
```

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

### DASH-19-S080 -- Stages

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:736` through `768`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `agent-runtime`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Stages

Reuses the Agent Composer stages, with additional:

**Additional Stage — Target specification.**

- What kinds of agents should this meta-agent produce?
- Target domain.
- Target parameters (ranges, constraints).
- Creation triggers: on-demand, scheduled, event-driven.

**Additional Stage — Agent-creation tools.**

- Which agent-creation operations is the meta-agent authorized to perform?
- Domain configuration permissions.
- Extension selection permissions.
- Gate configuration permissions.
- Registration permissions (can the meta-agent register children on-chain?).
- Budget permissions.

**Additional Stage — Child caveats.**

- What caveats must the meta-agent impose on its children?
- Inherited caveats from the meta-agent.
- Additional caveats for children.

**Additional Stage — Safety boundaries.**

- Max generation rate (children per day).
- Max concurrent children.
- Quality floor (children below this quality get auto-retired).
- Budget for meta-agent operations.
````

**Explicit detail extraction from this section:**

- Section hash: `b34b6c486ad9859e0bb2a9c6631dd9fd83a5fbff44b9a022f58a33108f5c7d12`

- **Additional Stage — Safety boundaries.**
- - Max generation rate (children per day). - Max concurrent children. - Quality floor (children below this quality get auto-retired). - Budget for meta-agent operations.

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.

- Runtime: wire into the production agent loop with lifecycle, cancellation, health, audit, provider/tool safety, and observable events.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

- [ ] Implement or verify `based` in the canonical crate with serde/validation/defaults where it is a data type, and unit tests for boundary behavior.
- [ ] For this dashboard-derived task, prefer backend projection/realtime support over frontend stitching: one stable payload, typed stale/degraded states, and fixtures for local UI development.

### DASH-19-S089 -- Stages

**Source section:** `/Users/will/dev/nunchi/nunchi-dashboard/docs/prd/19-authoring-surfaces.md:805` through `838`
**Heading level:** H3
**Concern split:** `api`, `realtime`, `storage`, `auth`, `chain`, `dashboard-support`, `verification`, `config-deployment`
**Implementation status:** `[ ] not started` `[ ] in progress` `[ ] implemented` `[ ] verified`

**Full source context for this task:**

````markdown
### Stages

**Stage 1 — Target type.**

What type of object does this generator produce?

- Arena.
- Eval.
- Domain.
- Gate.
- Extension.
- Custom (user-defined type).

**Stage 2 — Starting point.**

Template generators for the chosen type.

**Stage 3 — Generation logic.**

How does the generator produce an instance?

- From parameters: take input parameters, produce an instance.
- Procedural: use an algorithm to construct.
- LLM-assisted: use an LLM as part of the construction (but per thesis 4, the output must still be measured against external ground truth).

**Stage 4 — Quality criteria.**

How are the generator's outputs measured? Which evals apply?

**Stage 5 — Publish.**

- Publish to Generator Studio.
- Register on-chain.
````

**Explicit detail extraction from this section:**

- Section hash: `c8a8769339bde41a8ba0e5a2c08f45540a9e43154d58fb870b9c90506a9bfd42`

- **Stage 4 — Quality criteria.**

- - Publish to Generator Studio. - Register on-chain.

**Concern contracts to satisfy:**
- API: define concrete request/response structs, auth scope, error shape, pagination/filtering, idempotency semantics, and generated API-reference/OpenAPI coverage.
- Realtime: emit typed Bus/StateHub events with room, sequence, actor, timestamp, replay/degraded semantics, and dashboard cache-update payload.
- Storage: define deterministic schema, migration/default behavior, corruption handling, restart recovery, cleanup/GC, and fixture data.
- Security: enforce scope/capability checks before side effects, cover unauthenticated/read/write/admin/agent-token/JWT cases, and redact secrets.
- Chain: default to devnet/simulation, prove contract/client/indexer sync, enforce wallet/risk/simulation gates, and record audit/witness data.
- Frontend support: provide stable projections, mock fixtures, empty/loading/stale/degraded/unauthorized states, and realtime update payloads that match projection schemas.
- Verification: add deterministic unit, integration, route/CLI, auth/safety, persistence/restart, serialization, and failing-path tests; avoid LLM self-grading where ground truth is required.
- Config/deployment: validate defaults, hot reload or restart-required paths, env var diagnostics, service dry-runs, and non-destructive operations.

**Explicit implementation obligations derived from this section:**
- [ ] Implement or verify `of` in the canonical crate with serde/validation/defaults where it is a data type, and unit tests for boundary behavior.
- [ ] For this dashboard-derived task, prefer backend projection/realtime support over frontend stitching: one stable payload, typed stale/degraded states, and fixtures for local UI development.

**General implementation checklist:**
- [ ] Add one parity-ledger row for this exact source section with task id, source hash, owner, targets, tests, and acceptance gate.
- [ ] Extend existing canonical modules before adding new modules; document any new module choice in the ledger.
- [ ] Implement production-path wiring across runtime/service/storage/API/events, not only structs or mocks.
- [ ] Add dashboard-friendly projection and realtime payloads when any UI, operator, or PRD requirement would otherwise need to stitch state client-side.
- [ ] Persist durable state under existing storage conventions and cover restart/replay/recovery behavior.
- [ ] Add tests for happy path, invalid input, invalid state, unauthorized access, dependency unavailable, persistence/restart, serialization, and degraded frontend state where applicable.
- [ ] Update generated API/CLI/docs references or record an explicit no-public-surface rationale.
- [ ] Run verification commands and attach produced gate artifacts to the ledger row.

**Verification commands:**

```bash

cargo test -p roko-runtime
cargo test -p roko-gate
cargo test -p roko-chain
cd contracts && forge test
cargo test -p roko-cli
./target/debug/roko parity check --strict --area dashboard-prd/19-authoring-surfaces
```
